key_insight: OpenGEM26 expands molecular ML training data with 4.4 million DFT conformations, enabling GPTFF-mol to learn accurate force fields for diverse organic molecules including S/Cl-containing systems.
engineering_relevance: The model offers a faster surrogate for DFT in molecular simulation, supporting efficient geometry optimization, dynamics, and reaction-path studies in organic materials and drug-like chemistry.
method: Built OpenGEM26 with 200,000 unique molecules and full optimization trajectories
Computed structures at ωB97X-D/Def2-SVP and Def2-TZVP with dispersion corrections
Trained a graph neural network potential (GPTFF-mol) and benchmarked it against ANI-2x
evidence_signal: The abstract reports 4.4 million conformations, broader coverage than QM9 in energies/bond metrics, and an energy MAE of 16 meV/molecule (0.82 meV/atom) with improved force prediction over ANI-2x.
watch_item: Performance is shown for molecules with up to ten heavy atoms, so transfer to larger, highly flexible, or chemically out-of-distribution systems may require additional validation.
paper_title: Graph Neural Network Force Fields (GPTFF-mol) for Organic Molecules from Optimization Trajectories (OpenGEM26)
